regions: Latin America, Canada and the Caribbean (LACC)

dependencies: Third-party service providers for acquiring and servicing customers
Third-party processors and merchant acquirers (OptBlue partners)
Joint venture partners in China, Middle East, and Switzerland
Cobrand partners (Delta Air Lines, Hilton, Marriott, British Airways)
Network partners in approximately 120 countries and territories
Prepaid reloadable and gift card program manager
risks: Reliance on third-party service providers for critical operations
Disruptions or failures at third-party providers could interrupt services
Third parties may act inconsistently with company interests
Operational disruptions at partners (e.g., prepaid card business interruptions)
Concentration risk with key partners like Delta Air Lines
Loss of cobrand relationships could materially impact business
Third-party oversight subject to heightened regulatory scrutiny

regulatory_risks: Comprehensive banking regulation by Federal Reserve and OCC
Enhanced prudential standards as Category III firm (capital, liquidity, stress testing)
Potential Basel III capital rule changes could significantly increase capital requirements
Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) oversight and enforcement
CFPB proposed rule lowering credit card late fee safe harbor to $8
CFPB and DOT inquiries into credit card and airline rewards programs
Small business credit data collection and reporting requirements (effective July 2025)
Personal financial data rights rule requiring data sharing with third parties (April 2026)
Payments regulation including interchange fee caps in EU, Australia, Canada
State laws prohibiting interchange on sales tax and gratuities
Merchant surcharging permitted in certain jurisdictions (EU, UK, Australia, Canada, some US states)
EU and UK GDPR compliance with potential fines up to 4% of revenue
Data localization requirements in various countries (India, China)
Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Counter-Financing of Terrorism (CFT) compliance
Economic sanctions compliance (OFAC, EU, UK sanctions regimes)
Anti-corruption laws (FCPA, UK Bribery Act)
Climate-related risk management principles from federal banking regulators
Artificial intelligence and machine learning regulation (EU AI Act, Colorado AI Act)
Community Reinvestment Act (CRA) requirements
Compensation practices oversight by Federal Reserve and OCC
Resolution and recovery planning requirements
Orderly Liquidation Authority (OLA) potential application
Uncertainty regarding cobrand relationships in EU due to interchange fee regulation
Value-added tax (VAT) challenges in multiple countries
Merchant category code regulations
Transaction data usage restrictions
Fair access laws
Interest rate cap proposals

sanction_risks: Russia-Ukraine conflict led to suspension of operations in Russia and Belarus
Economic sanctions and export controls impact operations
Iran sanctions compliance - identified ATM transactions and accounts related to Iranian entities
Voluntary reporting to OFAC regarding Iran-related transactions
Potential retaliatory actions against companies due to geopolitical tensions
Sanctions activity subject to heightened regulatory scrutiny
Association with sanctioned persons, entities, governments could result in penalties
